summary: Opinion piece by Carole Herder on how shoeing reduces hoof circulation and causes laminitic separation, urging owners to learn what a natural hoof looks like.
Carole Herder reflects on horse trainer Warwick Schiller's videos and how changing her own attitude and intention restored connection and responsiveness in her horse.
History of horseshoeing: metal shoes began ~1500 years ago to lift hooves out of fetid stabling, while Xenophon's army and Mongols rode barefoot in tough terrain.
Guide to common challenges when transitioning a horse to barefoot, including social criticism, terrain sensitivity, hoof-wall chipping and occasional abscessing.
Holiday gift guide from Cavallo featuring Simple Boots, Total Comfort System saddle pads, Break-Away Halters, Cavallo Sheeps, Flumes and the Cooler Chair.
Roundup of post-Christmas trail riding events via ACTHA and a beach-riding cruise to Grand Cayman, Jamaica and Haiti through Best of America by Horseback.
Carole Herder on gratitude in horsemanship, citing barefoot puissance winner Luca Moneta and urging owners to thank their horses with a withers scratch.
New Year's post encouraging horse owners to spend more time with their horses or finally transition to barefoot, with a nudge toward Cavallo hoof boots.
Summary of an interview with hoof researcher Dr. Robert Bowker on holistic horse care, emphasizing that a simple diet of quality hay, salt and minerals matters.
Carole Herder explains training patterns (figure-eight, lateral flexion, Paddock Paradise track systems) as tools to refocus a nervous horse and teach new lessons.
Light post noting 2014 as the Chinese zodiac Year of the Horse, listing horse-year birth ranges and personality traits associated with the sign.
Willis Lamm's narrative essay on riding among wild horses in Nevada's Virginia Range, describing an encounter with a stallion-led band and the region's terrain.
